headline: New Poll Shows 67% Of Voters Can't Remember Who Their Senator Is, Blames TikTok
summary: Survey respondents were more successful at naming all five members of their favorite K-pop group than their elected officials.
fullBody: A new poll conducted by the Institute of Questionable Statistics has revealed that 67% of American voters cannot correctly identify their own senator, with 23% believing their senator is 'that guy from the car insurance commercials.' The survey found that voters were significantly more successful at naming celebrity relationships, TikTok trends, and the complete discography of obscure 90s bands than they were at naming their elected representatives. One respondent said, 'I know more about the drama between those two people on Love Island than I do about who represents me in Congress, and honestly, I care about both equally.' The poll also found that 89% of 18-24 year olds could name the current season's Bachelor contestants but only 12% could name their congressional representatives. Researchers recommend installing a 'representative reminder' app on all smartphones.
imagePrompt: A person holding a smartphone showing a voting booth next to a TikTok video, looking confused, comic illustration, flat design, blue and pink colors
